name: Hare, Josh

evidence: Josh Hare founded Hops & Grain Brewing in East Austin in 2011 and later launched Rick's Near Beer; a former pro triathlete, Rogue Running co-founder, and 7th-grade science teacher before brewing full time. No federal PAC contributions or TX lobbyist registration found.

name: Eckstein, Deece
resolved_employer: Retired Intergovernmental Relations Officer, Travis County (retired 2019); former chief of staff to state Sen. Rodney Ellis and senior advisor to Gov. Ann Richards; founder, Johnson Creek Consultancy
evidence: Deece Eckstein created and ran Travis County's intergovernmental relations (legislative advocacy) office 2008-2019; prior career as a Texas legislative/advocacy figure including 6 years as chief of staff to Sen. Rodney Ellis (D) and 3 years as senior advisor to Gov. Ann Richards (D). Now retired in Austin (78703).

affiliations.snippet: TEC lobby registration lists Eckstein, Deece (ID 00026158) registered 02/08/2005 with client 'People For the American Way,' a progressive advocacy org, compensation $50,000-$99,999.

name: Delgado, Alejandro

evidence: Most prominent public match is an AISD administrator who leads a family/enrollment support office; a Bowie HS graduate living in South Austin (zip 78749 is South Austin near Bowie) with two children in AISD elementary schools. Common name, so identity not certain, but AISD + South Austin geography fits.

name: Clark, Meagan
resolved_employer: Realtor — listed as Keller Williams; current public records show eXp Realty / Adelante Real Estate Team (TX license 0625584). Realtors change brokerages; likely same person.
evidence: Austin realtor Meagan Clark, TX real estate license 0625584, NAR member with GRI designation, works on the Adelante Real Estate Team (with mentor/mother Pam Bergara). Brokerage of record differs (eXp vs Keller Williams) but consistent Austin realtor identity.

name: Spearman, Joah
resolved_employer: Founder & CEO of Localeur (travel-recommendation startup); more recently founder/CEO of TenYour, a mission-driven fintech. Prior: director of operations at Bazaarvoice.
evidence: Well-documented Austin tech founder-CEO (Localeur), one of few Black founders to raise >$5M without institutional VC. Active civic/nonprofit board leader in Austin; 2022 Austin City Council District 9 candidate.

affiliations.snippet: Serves as membership chair for the board of directors of ZACH Theatre, the leading performing arts theatre in Central Texas.
Previously served as chairman of the board of directors for AIDS Services of Austin.

affiliations.role: Former board executive committee member (two terms)
affiliations.snippet: Served two terms on the executive committee of the board of directors for KLRU-TV, Austin's PBS affiliate.

affiliations.role: Former vice chair (Music Commission); Downtown Commission member
affiliations.snippet: Served as vice chair of the Austin Music Commission, which advised the Mayor and City Council, and also served on the Downtown Commission.

name: Sandoval, Vero

evidence: Veronica 'Vero' Sandoval, raised in East Austin (zip 78723 is East Austin — matches), works on UFCU's Financial Success Program bringing financial tools to underserved communities. Also served as Education Co-chair of Frida Friday ATX's 2024 planning committee.

affiliations.snippet: Education Co-chair for Frida Friday ATX's 2024 Planning and Programming Committee (a WoC/BIPOC-centered Austin marketplace and cultural event).

evidence: Lyndsey Garcia is Director of Advocacy at Hawai'i REALTORS in Honolulu, a public-policy/government-relations expert who has testified before the Hawaii legislature on housing and property-tax matters. Honolulu (zip 96813) donor to challenger Shah.

affiliations.role: Director of Advocacy (real-estate industry government relations)
affiliations.snippet: Director of Advocacy focusing on legislative analysis and government relations for the state realtor association; has testified before Hawaii's legislature.
